title: Tilray Brands, Inc. (TLRY) Acquires BrewDog
summary:
Tilray Brands, Inc. (NASDAQ:TLRY) has made a strategic acquisition of BrewDog assets for £33 million, which includes the global brand, intellectual property, and UK brewing operations, along with 11 brewpubs located in key cities across the UK and Ireland. This acquisition is expected to significantly enhance Tilray's beverage segment, contributing approximately $200 million in annual net revenue and an adjusted EBITDA of $6 million to $8 million. However, the company anticipates that the integration process will lead to a delay in EBITDA contributions for the final quarter of fiscal 2026, as well as some temporary revenue timing issues in early fiscal 2027 due to licensing transfers. 

The acquisition aligns with Tilray's broader strategy to expand its global beverage platform, which is projected to generate around $500 million in annual revenue, contributing to a consolidated annualized revenue target of approximately $1.2 billion. This move not only strengthens Tilray's position in the beverage market but also reflects its commitment to diversifying its portfolio beyond cannabis products. The company is actively pursuing additional deals for BrewDog properties in the United States and Australia, indicating a robust growth strategy in the beverage sector.

Tilray Brands operates in multiple segments, including cannabis, distribution, beverage, and wellness, positioning itself as a leader in consumer packaged goods. While the acquisition of BrewDog is a significant step for Tilray, analysts suggest that investors should also consider other sectors, such as AI stocks, which may offer greater upside potential with lower risk. As Tilray integrates BrewDog's operations, the market will be closely watching for how this impacts its overall financial performance and strategic direction in the coming years.
social_text: Tilray Brands, Inc. acquired BrewDog assets for £33 million, targeting $200 million in annual revenue. The deal boosts Tilray's beverage platform to $500 million in revenue, with ongoing negotiations for U.S. and Australian properties.

title: Innovation Beverage Group Limited (IBG) Announces an Update Regarding its Proposed Merger with BlockFuel Energy
summary:
Innovation Beverage Group Limited (IBG) has provided a significant update regarding its proposed merger with BlockFuel Energy, aiming for completion in the first quarter of 2026. This merger is designed to create a vertically integrated system that monetizes hydrocarbons through both traditional sales and innovative digital energy applications. As part of this initiative, BlockFuel has resumed production on ten wells and plans to restart an additional seven by the end of February 2026, which will enhance active output and increase available gas volumes. The anticipated first oil and gas sales are expected to occur in February, providing crucial cash flow visibility for the merged entity.

BlockFuel is also advancing plans to build modular gas-to-power and digital mining infrastructure, strategically located near wellheads. This infrastructure is expected to be cost-effective, with onsite gas-to-power costs projected to be lower than traditional grid prices, thus minimizing shipping and processing expenses. Furthermore, BlockFuel has signed a letter of intent to acquire approximately 4,000 contiguous acres of producing oil fields, which will expand its operational scale and improve gas output, thereby enhancing the overall profitability of the merger.

Innovation Beverage Group Limited is recognized for its diverse beverage portfolio, which includes 60 formulations across 13 brands, both alcoholic and non-alcoholic. While the potential of IBG as an investment is acknowledged, analysts suggest that certain AI stocks may present greater upside potential with reduced risk. The ongoing developments in the merger with BlockFuel Energy could position IBG favorably in the energy sector, aligning with trends in energy monetization and digital applications.
social_text: Innovation Beverage Group Limited is set to merge with BlockFuel Energy, targeting Q1 2026 completion. BlockFuel plans to resume production on 17 wells, enhancing cash flow and reducing costs with onsite gas-to-power solutions.

title: MGP Ingredients, Inc. (MGPI) Posts Fourth-Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results
summary:
MGP Ingredients, Inc. (NASDAQ:MGPI) has reported its fourth-quarter and full-year results for 2025, revealing significant financial challenges. The company experienced a notable decline in sales, with fourth-quarter revenues falling to $138.3 million, a 23% decrease compared to the previous year. This downturn was accompanied by a substantial net loss of $134.6 million, which included a $152.6 million non-cash impairment of goodwill and intangible assets. Adjusted net income also saw a sharp decline, dropping 60% to $13.7 million, while adjusted EBITDA fell by 51% to $26.1 million. The gross profit margin decreased to 34.9%, reflecting the overall struggles faced by the company in a competitive market environment.

For the fiscal year 2025, MGP Ingredients reported total sales of $536.4 million, a 24% decrease, leading to a net loss of $107.8 million. The company's adjusted net income decreased by 51% to $61.5 million, and adjusted EBITDA slid 41% to $116.0 million. Despite these setbacks, operating cash flow improved to $121.5 million, indicating some resilience in cash management. The decline in sales was particularly pronounced in the Distilling Solutions segment, which saw a staggering 47% drop, attributed to a 53% decrease in brown goods sales.

Looking ahead, MGP Ingredients has set a forecast for 2026, projecting sales between $480 million and $500 million, with adjusted EBITDA expected to range from $90 million to $98 million. The company operates across three primary segments: Distilling Solutions, Branded Spirits, and Ingredient Solutions, focusing on food, beverage, specialty wheat protein, and starch food ingredients. While MGP Ingredients remains a notable player in the alcohol sector, analysts suggest that other investment opportunities, particularly in AI stocks, may offer better growth potential and lower risk in the current market landscape.
social_text: MGP Ingredients reported Q4 sales of $138.3M (-23%) and a net loss of $134.6M, driven by a $152.6M impairment. FY 2025 sales fell 24% to $536.4M, with adjusted EBITDA down 41% to $116M. 2026 sales forecast: $480M-$500M.

title: Willamette Valley Vineyards, Inc. (WVVI) Announces Modifications in Distribution and Sales Tactics
summary:
Willamette Valley Vineyards, Inc. (WVVI) has announced significant modifications to its distribution and sales strategies, particularly focusing on the East Coast market. The winery has partnered with Republic National Distributing Company (RNDC) to enhance its distribution capabilities in New York State and the Mid-Atlantic region. This strategic alignment not only connects Maryland and the District of Columbia with existing operations in Virginia but also leverages RNDC's extensive network in New York, thereby expanding the winery's reach and accessibility to consumers in these key markets.

In addition to the distribution changes, WVVI has restructured its national sales division into five distinct regions, each led by experienced professionals in the wine industry. Robert Goodrich has been appointed as the Eastern Region Sales Director, bringing a wealth of experience from his previous role at Ste. Michelle Wine Estate. Meanwhile, Jimmy Lewis has taken on the role of California Sales Manager, contributing over three decades of expertise in vineyards and wholesale distribution. This restructuring aims to enhance sales performance and market penetration across the United States.

Willamette Valley Vineyards operates primarily in two segments: Direct Sales and Distributor Sales. The recent changes reflect the company's commitment to optimizing its sales strategies and distribution networks to better serve its customers and capitalize on growth opportunities. While the company shows promise as an investment, analysts suggest that other sectors, particularly AI stocks, may present greater potential for returns in the current market environment.
social_text: Willamette Valley Vineyards, Inc. has restructured its distribution by partnering with RNDC for New York and Mid-Atlantic regions, while expanding its sales team with industry veterans to enhance market reach and performance.

title: Is The Boston Beer Company, Inc. (SAM) Among the Best Alcohol Stocks to Buy?
summary:
The Boston Beer Company, Inc. (NYSE:SAM) is currently highlighted as one of the top alcohol stocks to consider for investment. Recently, the company announced an expansion of its Sinless Vodka Cocktails from three test markets to 34 states, showcasing a strategic move to increase its market presence. The revamped product features a 5% ABV vodka base, is low in calories at 100 per serving, and is free from sugar and carbs. This expansion includes a variety of flavors such as Cranberry, Pineapple, Black Cherry, and Peach, and will be available in multiple packaging formats, catering to diverse consumer preferences.

Financially, Boston Beer reported a decline in fourth-quarter net revenue, totaling $385.7 million, down from $402.3 million the previous year. The company experienced a loss of $22.5 million, or $2.12 per share, which is an improvement compared to the $38.8 million loss from the same quarter last year. Factors contributing to this downturn include a $3.6 million impact from tariffs and a 6% decrease in depletions, particularly in its popular brands like Truly Hard Seltzer and Samuel Adams. Despite these challenges, the company projects earnings per share between $8.50 and $11.00 for 2026, along with modest pricing increases and stable depletion rates.

While Boston Beer Company shows potential for growth, analysts suggest that other sectors, particularly AI stocks, may present better investment opportunities with higher upside potential and lower risk. The company continues to innovate and adapt to market demands, but investors are encouraged to weigh their options carefully, especially in light of the competitive landscape in the beverage industry and the ongoing economic factors affecting consumer spending.
social_text: Boston Beer Company (SAM) expands Sinless Vodka Cocktails to 34 states, featuring 5% ABV and 100 calories. Q4 revenue fell to $385.7M with a loss of $22.5M. EPS forecast for 2026 is $8.50-$11.00.

title: Barclays Retains an Equal Weight Rating on Ambev S.A. (ABEV)
summary:
Barclays has reaffirmed its Equal Weight rating on Ambev S.A. (NYSE:ABEV) while raising the price target from $2.50 to $3. This adjustment reflects the bank's focus on profitability-driven earnings and cash flow growth anticipated for 2026. The decision comes amidst a backdrop of mixed performance metrics for Ambev, which has shown resilience in revenue growth despite challenges in volume sales.

In its recent financial disclosures, Ambev reported a fourth-quarter organic net revenue growth of 4.8%, primarily fueled by an 8.7% increase in net revenue per hectoliter. However, total volume experienced a decline of 3.6%. For the full year, organic net revenue rose by 4.0%, with a notable 7.5% increase in net revenue per hectoliter, counterbalancing a 3.3% reduction in volume across its operations. The company also achieved a 5.6% increase in normalized EBITDA, enhancing its margin to 33.4%, marking three consecutive years of margin expansion.

Despite the positive revenue trends, Ambev's normalized profit for the fourth quarter fell by 8.0% to R$4.6 billion, while the full-year profit saw a modest increase of 1.6% to R$15.1 billion. Operating cash flow for the year was reported at R$24.5 billion, reflecting a 6.3% decline. While Barclays acknowledges Ambev's potential as an investment, it suggests that certain AI stocks may present greater upside opportunities with lower risk profiles, indicating a competitive landscape for investor attention.
social_text: Barclays raises Ambev's price target to $3, maintaining an Equal Weight rating. Q4 organic net revenue grew 4.8% despite a 3.6% volume drop. Full-year normalized profit reached R$15.1 billion, up 1.6%.

title: Molson Coors Beverage Company (TAP) Expects a Significant Dip in 2026 Profit
summary:
Molson Coors Beverage Company (TAP) is bracing for a notable decline in profits for 2026, primarily driven by rising aluminum tariffs and a decrease in consumption among price-sensitive consumers. The company anticipates adjusted earnings per share to drop by 11% to 15%, contrasting sharply with analyst expectations of a modest increase to $5.48. Following the announcement of disappointing fourth-quarter revenue results, TAP's shares fell approximately 6% in after-hours trading, highlighting investor concerns about the company's financial outlook. The firm projects net sales to either decline by 1% or increase by 1% in 2026, which is less optimistic than previous forecasts of a slight 0.1% reduction.

In the fourth quarter, Molson Coors reported net sales of $2.66 billion, which fell short of the anticipated $2.71 billion. However, underlying earnings of $1.21 per share exceeded expectations of $1.16. The increase in costs, particularly due to an 8.1% rise in the Midwest aluminum premium, has significantly impacted the cost of goods sold per hectoliter. CEO Rahul Goyal acknowledged the tough decisions ahead for the company as it seeks to navigate through weak demand and inflationary pressures that have persisted since 2025.

The company's leadership, including CFO Tracey Joubert, has indicated that commodity inflation will continue to be a major challenge for profitability in the upcoming year. Despite the current struggles, Molson Coors remains a significant player in the beverage industry, operating across the Americas, EMEA, and APAC regions. While TAP shows potential as an investment, analysts suggest that certain AI stocks may present better opportunities with lower risks, especially in light of ongoing economic challenges and market dynamics.
social_text: Molson Coors (TAP) expects a profit dip of 11-15% in 2026 due to rising aluminum tariffs and decreased consumer demand. Q4 sales were $2.66B, missing estimates of $2.71B, leading to a 6% share drop.

title: Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (BUD) Exceeds Fourth-quarter Profit and Revenue Projections
summary:
Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (NYSE:BUD) has reported impressive fourth-quarter results, surpassing profit and revenue expectations while experiencing smaller-than-anticipated volume declines. The company confirmed its profit growth target for 2026, aiming for an increase between 4% and 8%, which is notably higher than competitors Heineken and Carlsberg, who project growth of only 2% to 6%. The firm has invested significantly in sales and marketing, totaling $7.4 billion, and has successfully gained or maintained market share in two-thirds of its markets. CEO Michel Doukeris expressed optimism about the company's momentum as it heads into 2026, with major events like the Super Bowl and Winter Olympics expected to further enhance performance. Following the announcement, BUD shares rose by 2% in early trading, reflecting investor confidence in the company's strategic direction.

Despite these positive developments, Anheuser-Busch InBev's annual profit growth of 4.9% fell short of its own projections and marked a decline from the more than 8% growth seen in 2024. The company has faced challenges including weak global demand, which has been exacerbated by limited consumer spending and adverse weather conditions. Additionally, the company reported a significant 38.7% drop in quarterly earnings from China, attributed to declining sales and the costs associated with revitalizing its business in the region. Foreign exchange fluctuations and U.S. aluminum tariffs have also contributed to increased operational expenses, complicating the company's recovery efforts.

Anheuser-Busch InBev operates as a holding company for a diverse portfolio of alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages across various regions, including North America, South America, and Asia Pacific. While the company shows potential as an investment, analysts suggest that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside with less risk. The ongoing challenges in key markets and the need for strategic adjustments highlight the importance of monitoring BUD's performance closely as it navigates a complex global landscape.
social_text: Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) exceeded Q4 profit and revenue forecasts, targeting 4%-8% growth for 2026. Despite a 4.9% annual profit rise, challenges in China and tariffs impact performance. Shares up 2%.

title: Constellation Brands, Inc. (STZ) Names Nicholas Fink as its New CEO
summary:
Constellation Brands, Inc. (NYSE:STZ) has appointed Nicholas Fink as its new CEO, effective April 13, 2026. Fink succeeds Bill Newlands, who has been with the company since 2015 and served as CEO since 2019. Newlands will transition to a strategic advisor role for several months to ensure a smooth leadership change. This appointment comes at a critical time for Constellation, as the company faces challenges in alcohol sales, particularly among Hispanic consumers who have reduced their purchases due to economic concerns and political factors stemming from the Trump administration's immigration policies.

Nicholas Fink brings a wealth of experience to his new role, having previously overseen significant organizational changes at Fortune Brands Innovations. Analysts, including TD Cowen's Robert Moskow, believe that Fink's familiarity with the company and his board experience will help maintain the strategic direction of Constellation Brands during this transitional period. The company operates across four segments: Beer, Wine and Spirits, Corporate Operations and Other, and Canopy, positioning it as a key player in the alcohol market.

Despite the challenges facing Constellation Brands, there remains potential for growth and investment. While some analysts suggest that certain AI stocks may offer better upside potential, the leadership change could signal a new strategic approach for STZ. Investors are encouraged to monitor the company's performance closely as Fink takes the helm and implements his vision for the future of Constellation Brands.
social_text: Constellation Brands appoints Nicholas Fink as CEO effective April 13, 2026, succeeding Bill Newlands amid weak alcohol sales. Fink's experience at Fortune Brands Innovations may stabilize strategic direction.

title: Brown-Forman Corporation (BF-B) and Pabst Brewing Company Sign a Deal to Produce Flavored Malt Beverages in the U.S.
summary:
Brown-Forman Corporation and Pabst Brewing Company have announced a significant shift in their partnership regarding flavored malt beverages in the U.S. Effective July 7, 2026, Brown-Forman will take over the supply, sales, marketing, and distribution of these products, which includes popular offerings like Jack Daniel's Country Cocktails and other flavored malt beverages. This transition comes after a 2021 agreement that granted Pabst exclusive rights to produce these beverages. Robinson Brown IV, a senior executive at Brown-Forman, emphasized that this move will enhance the company's control over its ready-to-drink strategy, particularly as consumer demand continues to rise for these products.

The decision to bring the flavored malt beverage brands in-house aligns with Brown-Forman's broader strategy to streamline operations and focus on innovation within its portfolio. Meanwhile, Pabst Brewing Company will redirect its efforts towards its core products and new innovations, as stated by CEO Greig DeBow. This strategic realignment reflects the evolving landscape of the beverage industry, where companies are increasingly looking to optimize their offerings and respond to changing consumer preferences.

In addition to this partnership change, Brown-Forman's board has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.2310 per share for both Class A and Class B common stock, reinforcing its commitment to returning value to shareholders. This dividend is set to be paid to shareholders of record on March 9, 2026, further solidifying Brown-Forman's position as a stable investment in the alcohol sector. The company's diverse portfolio includes well-known brands such as Jack Daniel's, Woodford Reserve, and El Jimador, positioning it favorably in the competitive beverage market.
social_text: Brown-Forman will take over the production and distribution of flavored malt beverages from Pabst Brewing, enhancing control over its ready-to-drink strategy amid rising consumer demand. A quarterly dividend of $0.2310 per share is also announced.

summary:

title: U.S. crude oil tops $80 per barrel as escalating Iran war disrupts global fuel supplies
summary:
U.S. crude oil prices have surged past $80 per barrel, driven by escalating tensions in the Iran war that have severely disrupted global fuel supplies. The price of West Texas Intermediate oil experienced a significant increase of 8.51%, closing at $81.01 per barrel, marking the largest single-day gain since May 2020. Meanwhile, the global benchmark Brent crude rose by 4.93% to settle at $85.41 per barrel. This week alone, U.S. oil prices have jumped approximately 21%, reflecting the market's reaction to the ongoing geopolitical instability in the region.

The conflict has led to a standstill in tanker traffic through the crucial Strait of Hormuz, a vital passageway for about 20% of global oil consumption. The situation escalated when Iran reportedly attacked an oil tanker with a missile, prompting the Revolutionary Guard to threaten further actions against vessels in the strait. In response to the heightened risks, the Trump administration has indicated plans for political risk insurance and naval escorts for tankers, although no timeline has been provided for when the strait will be safe for commercial shipping.

As a direct consequence of these developments, retail gasoline prices in the U.S. have surged nearly 27 cents in just one week, averaging $3.25 per gallon. This spike in fuel prices mirrors previous surges seen during significant geopolitical events, such as the Russian invasion of Ukraine. With the situation in the Middle East remaining volatile, the potential for further disruptions to oil supplies looms large, impacting both consumers and the broader economy significantly.
social_text: U.S. crude oil prices soared to $81.01 per barrel, an 8.51% increase, as the Iran war disrupts global supplies. Retail gas prices jumped 27 cents to $3.25 per gallon, echoing past crises like the Ukraine invasion.

title: War escalation, jobs report fallout, and Oracle earnings: What to watch this week
summary:
This week, U.S. equities faced significant declines, primarily driven by escalating tensions in Iran and a subsequent spike in global oil prices. The S&P 500 dropped 1.3% on Friday, marking a 1.5% loss for the year, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell approximately 450 points, resulting in a 1.2% year-to-date decline. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite also suffered, with a 1.6% drop on Friday and a total loss of around 3.7% since the beginning of the year. The widespread market downturn has raised concerns about the overall health of the economy, as investors grapple with the implications of rising energy costs and their potential to trigger inflationary pressures.

Market analysts, including Goldman Sachs' head of oil research Daan Struyven, have warned that the effects of the ongoing conflict are nonlinear, suggesting that prolonged energy shocks could exacerbate market volatility. The upcoming week will be critical for investors, who are eager to understand the duration of these economic challenges. Key economic indicators, such as the Consumer Price Index and the Job Openings and Labor Turnover survey, are set to be released, providing insights into inflation trends and labor market conditions.

In addition to macroeconomic factors, corporate earnings reports will also be in focus, with Oracle's earnings announcement on Tuesday expected to draw significant attention. As the market navigates these turbulent waters, investors will be closely monitoring both economic data and corporate performance to gauge the potential for recovery or further decline in the coming weeks.
social_text: U.S. equities fell sharply as the Iran conflict escalated, causing oil prices to surge. The S&P 500 is down 1.5% YTD, while the Dow lost 1.2%. Key economic indicators and Oracle's earnings report are upcoming.

title: Bond traders already had their hands full, ‘then a war breaks out’
summary:
The recent outbreak of war in the Middle East has significantly impacted global financial markets, particularly the bond sector. Daniel Ivascyn, the chief investment officer at Pacific Investment Management Co. (Pimco), noted that even before the conflict, there were already concerns regarding corporate credit and the effects of artificial intelligence on market stability. As a result, he had begun to adjust his investment strategy by reducing exposure to corporate credit and increasing cash-equivalent holdings, while maintaining a preference for medium-dated Treasuries. However, the escalation of hostilities has introduced new uncertainties that complicate these strategies.

The conflict, particularly the US-Israeli attack on Iran, has led to a surge in crude oil prices, which in turn has driven yields on US government bonds higher. This shift has raised inflation fears at a time when prices are already above central bank targets. Ivascyn remarked on the market's reaction, indicating that instead of serving as a safe haven, Treasuries have become more volatile due to these geopolitical tensions. The situation is further complicated by a recent report showing a surprising drop in US payrolls, which has raised concerns about potential stagflation in the economy.

As the war continues, the implications for the $31 trillion US bond market are profound. Investors who had anticipated a straightforward strategy of collecting interest and awaiting interest rate cuts from the Federal Reserve now face a more complex landscape. With rising risks and multiple factors influencing market dynamics, the bond market is navigating through a period of heightened uncertainty, making it essential for investors to remain vigilant and adaptable in their strategies amidst ongoing geopolitical developments.
social_text: Daniel Ivascyn of Pimco adjusts strategies amid Middle East conflict, impacting the $31T US bond market. Rising crude prices push yields up, raising inflation fears and complicating investment plans for 2026.

title: The AI trade still 'has legs': Wall Street analysts weigh tech stock picks amid market sell-off
summary:
In the wake of escalating US-Israeli tensions and rising oil prices, Wall Street analysts are reassessing their tech stock recommendations amid a market sell-off. The geopolitical climate has prompted investors to reevaluate risk exposure, particularly in sectors that may be more resilient during economic downturns. Analysts emphasize that certain tech giants, notably Microsoft and Apple, are positioned as safe havens due to their essential products and strong financials. Microsoft, with a staggering $625 billion backlog, and Apple, boasting significant cash flow, are seen as critical anchors in a volatile market environment.

Rob Haworth from US Bank Wealth Management highlights a 'structural tailwind' for the AI sector, predicting a 30% increase in hyperscaler investments by 2026. This optimism suggests that the AI trade will continue to thrive despite broader market uncertainties. Analysts like Dan Ives and Gil Luria reinforce this sentiment, indicating that consumer demand for products like iPhones and enterprise solutions like Windows and Azure will persist even in economic slowdowns.

While Microsoft and Apple are viewed as stable options, Alphabet is also recognized for its steady business model. However, analysts express caution regarding Meta, which relies heavily on advertising revenue, making it vulnerable to budget cuts from small and medium-sized businesses. Conversely, Amazon is gaining attention for its retail and cloud service margins, with analysts noting it is currently the most attractive on a sum-of-the-parts basis. This analysis underscores a strategic shift in investment focus towards tech companies that can weather economic storms effectively.
social_text: Wall Street analysts see resilience in tech stocks amid market volatility, with Microsoft and Apple as top picks. Microsoft has a $625B backlog, while AI investments are set to rise 30% by 2026, signaling strong growth potential.

title: ‘If they rise, they rise’: Trump has zero concern for spiking US gas prices  — says Iran operation ‘far more important’
summary:
In a recent interview, former President Donald Trump expressed a lack of concern regarding the rising gas prices in the U.S., emphasizing that the situation in Iran is of greater importance. His comments come amidst a significant crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial passage for global oil transport, where production cuts could reach 6 million barrels per day if the situation escalates. The closure of this strategic waterway has already led to Iraq halting 1.5 million barrels per day of production and Kuwait reducing output due to storage limitations. Energy experts warn that crude oil prices could soar to $150 per barrel if tankers are unable to navigate through the Strait, which carries about 20% of the world's oil supply daily.

Despite the alarming rise in gas prices, which have surpassed $3.40 per gallon—the highest since September 2024—Trump remains focused on the geopolitical implications of the U.S. and Israel's military actions against Iran. He dismissed the need to tap into the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, a move he previously criticized when it was employed by President Biden. Internally, however, White House officials are reportedly concerned about the political ramifications of rising fuel costs, with Chief of Staff Susie Wiles warning that inaction could be disastrous for Republican candidates in the upcoming midterm elections.

The Biden administration is actively engaging with oil executives to explore potential solutions to mitigate the impact of soaring gas prices. As the situation develops, the balance between addressing domestic energy concerns and navigating international tensions remains a critical challenge for U.S. policymakers. With the potential for further escalation in the Middle East, the energy market is poised for volatility, which could have lasting effects on both prices and political landscapes in the U.S.
social_text: Trump downplays rising gas prices, stating Iran's situation is more critical. Gas prices exceed $3.40/gallon, with crude potentially hitting $150/barrel if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. Production cuts could reach 6 million barrels/day.

title: Top 2 Growth Stocks to Buy After Nvidia's Latest Sell-Off
summary:
Nvidia's recent earnings report, released on February 26, showcased strong financial performance; however, the stock experienced a notable decline of over 9% by February 27. Despite a slight recovery, Nvidia shares remain below their pre-earnings levels, prompting investors to reassess their strategies. The shift in focus is moving away from immediate results towards the sustainability of artificial intelligence (AI) capital expenditures, as competitive pressures in the semiconductor industry intensify. This evolving landscape has led investors to consider alternative sectors, particularly energy stocks, which are expected to thrive amid rising electricity demand.

Among the energy stocks gaining attention are Constellation Energy and GE Vernova. Constellation Energy, following its acquisition of Calpine in January 2026, has solidified its position as a leading electricity producer in the U.S. The merger has significantly enhanced its generation capacity to 55 gigawatts and expanded its customer base to nearly 2.5 million. This strategic move positions Constellation to capitalize on the increasing demand for electricity, especially from data centers, while remaining insulated from geopolitical oil supply disruptions.

GE Vernova is also highlighted as a promising growth stock, benefiting from the long-term rise in U.S. electricity demand. As the energy sector adapts to changing market dynamics, both Constellation Energy and GE Vernova are well-positioned to leverage their strengths in the domestic power markets. Investors are encouraged to explore these opportunities as they navigate the current volatility in the semiconductor and AI sectors, seeking stability and growth in energy investments.
social_text: Nvidia's stock fell over 9% post-earnings despite strong results, shifting investor focus to energy stocks like Constellation Energy and GE Vernova, poised to benefit from rising U.S. electricity demand and insulated from oil disruptions.

title: Why Okta's Shares Tumbled 14% Last Month
summary:
In February, shares of Okta (NASDAQ: OKTA), a prominent player in the identity and access management cybersecurity sector, experienced a significant decline of 14.2%. This drop was largely attributed to the introduction of a new security tool by Anthropic, an artificial intelligence company known for its chatbot Claude. The tool, named Claude Code Security, is designed to scan computer code for vulnerabilities and suggest targeted patches, raising concerns among investors about the potential disruption AI could cause to established cybersecurity firms like Okta. The news sent shockwaves through the market, affecting not only Okta but also other cybersecurity stocks, as investors grappled with the implications of AI advancements on traditional software security solutions.

The launch of Claude Code Security has been positioned as a game-changer in the cybersecurity landscape, as it promises to identify more subtle vulnerabilities that existing tools may overlook. Currently available only to a select group of Anthropic's Enterprise and Team customers, the tool is expected to be rolled out more broadly in the future. This has led to heightened anxiety among investors regarding the viability of traditional cybersecurity companies in an increasingly AI-driven market. The fear of obsolescence has prompted a reevaluation of the cybersecurity sector, with many investors questioning the long-term sustainability of companies like Okta.

Despite the current turmoil, experts suggest that it is premature to declare the end of cybersecurity companies. The demand for robust security solutions is unlikely to diminish, as organizations continue to face evolving threats. While AI may introduce new challenges, it also has the potential to enhance cybersecurity measures. Companies like Okta may need to adapt and innovate in response to these changes, but the fundamental need for cybersecurity remains strong. As the industry evolves, it will be crucial for cybersecurity firms to leverage AI technologies to stay competitive and address the growing complexities of digital security.
social_text: Okta's shares fell 14.2% in February after Anthropic launched Claude Code Security, a tool that scans code for vulnerabilities. Investors fear AI's disruptive potential on cybersecurity firms like Okta.

title: Cellebrite DI Expands Case-to-Closure Platform With AI Tools, Corellium and Drone Forensics Update
summary:
Cellebrite DI is enhancing its Case-to-Closure platform by integrating advanced AI tools and updates for Corellium and drone forensics, as discussed by CFO Dave Barter at a recent Morgan Stanley conference. The company emphasizes the importance of mobile devices in investigations, highlighting their role as a rich source of information. Barter explained that Cellebrite's technology can exploit vulnerabilities to access data from various devices, including Android and iOS, even when passwords are not available. This capability is crucial for law enforcement agencies that often require more than just locally stored data, as cloud services linked to devices can provide essential evidence.

Cellebrite's offerings are categorized into three main areas within the Case-to-Closure platform: digital forensic software, evidence management, and analytics. The Inseyets product is particularly notable for its ability to access devices, extract data, and transform it into actionable investigative insights. The company primarily serves state and local law enforcement, with enterprise clients making up less than 10% of its revenue. As part of its strategic shift, Cellebrite is moving towards a subscription and consumption-based pricing model, which is expected to enhance revenue growth and customer retention.

The company reported a gross retention rate of 91%, with a target of 92% to 93%, and is aiming for an 18% to 19% revenue growth while maintaining free cash flow margins above 30%. The transition to subscription pricing is already underway, with approximately 55% of Inseyets users migrating to this model. This strategic pivot not only positions Cellebrite to better meet the evolving needs of law enforcement agencies but also aligns with broader trends in the software industry towards subscription-based revenue models.
social_text: Cellebrite DI expands its Case-to-Closure platform with AI tools and drone forensics updates, targeting 18%-19% revenue growth and maintaining >30% free cash flow margins, primarily serving law enforcement agencies.

title: Jack Dorsey displeased with stablecoin push
summary:
Jack Dorsey, the co-founder of Twitter and the fintech company Block, has expressed his dissatisfaction with the growing demand for stablecoins within his company. Dorsey, a staunch advocate for Bitcoin, believes that the cryptocurrency should serve as a decentralized financial solution independent of traditional financial systems. He has integrated Bitcoin into various Block products, including Cash App and Bitkey, and has made bold predictions about Bitcoin's future value, suggesting it could reach $1 million by 2030. Dorsey emphasizes that Bitcoin should not only be a store of value but should also have practical use cases to encourage wider adoption.

In a recent interview with Wired, Dorsey elaborated on Block's commitment to Bitcoin, stating that the company aims to provide an open protocol for money transmission. He believes that Bitcoin is the most effective decentralized protocol available, as it is not controlled by any single entity. However, he acknowledged the pressure from customers who are increasingly requesting stablecoin support, which he finds disappointing.

Dorsey's concerns reflect a broader tension within the cryptocurrency industry, where the rise of stablecoins poses challenges to the original vision of Bitcoin as a decentralized currency. While he recognizes the market demand for stablecoins, he remains committed to promoting Bitcoin's unique qualities and advocating for a de minimis tax exemption for everyday Bitcoin transactions. This situation highlights the ongoing evolution of the cryptocurrency landscape and the balancing act that companies like Block must perform to meet customer needs while staying true to their foundational principles.
social_text: Jack Dorsey, founder of Block, expresses displeasure over stablecoin integration despite Bitcoin's strong demand. He advocates for Bitcoin's use as a decentralized financial protocol, aiming for $1 million by 2030.

title: TriZetto confirms 3.4M people’s health and personal data was stolen during breach
summary:
TriZetto, a prominent health tech company owned by Cognizant, has confirmed a significant data breach affecting over 3.4 million individuals. The breach, which went undetected for nearly a year, involved the theft of sensitive personal and health information, including names, dates of birth, home addresses, Social Security numbers, and insurance eligibility transaction reports. This incident raises serious concerns about the security measures in place at TriZetto, which serves approximately 200 million people across 875,000 healthcare providers in the U.S. The breach was identified on October 2, 2025, but investigations revealed that hackers had access to the company's servers as early as November 2024, indicating a prolonged vulnerability that could have severe implications for affected individuals and healthcare providers alike.

The ramifications of this breach extend beyond just TriZetto, as several healthcare organizations, including OCHIN, a nonprofit consultancy serving rural and community care providers, have confirmed that their patients' information was compromised. This incident highlights the interconnected nature of healthcare technology and the potential risks involved when patient data is not adequately protected. The breach underscores the urgent need for enhanced cybersecurity measures within the healthcare sector, particularly as cyberattacks become increasingly sophisticated and prevalent.

Cognizant's spokesperson, William Abelson, stated that the company has since eliminated the threat but did not provide details on why it took so long to detect the breach. This lack of transparency raises further questions about the effectiveness of their security protocols and the overall readiness of healthcare technology firms to combat cyber threats. As the industry grapples with the fallout from this breach, it is imperative for organizations to reassess their cybersecurity strategies to safeguard sensitive patient information and restore trust in their systems.
social_text: TriZetto confirms a data breach impacting 3.4M individuals, with hackers accessing sensitive health and personal data since Nov 2024. The breach raises urgent concerns about cybersecurity in healthcare technology.

title: Mammoth Energy reports Q4 cont ops EPS (26c) vs. (20c) last year
summary:
Mammoth Energy has reported its Q4 results, revealing a continuing operations earnings per share (EPS) of 26 cents, an improvement from a loss of 20 cents in the same quarter last year. The company's revenue for the quarter was reported at $9.5 million, slightly down from $10 million in the previous year. Mark Layton, the CFO, emphasized that 2025 was a transformative year for the company, marked by strategic divestitures that generated over $150 million in cash. These moves have not only strengthened Mammoth's balance sheet but also allowed for reinvestment into higher-growth opportunities, particularly in their aviation platform, which received over $65 million in funding. This investment is seen as a crucial step towards scaling the business and enhancing long-term shareholder value.

In addition to the financial maneuvers, Layton acknowledged that operational execution in Q4 did not meet the company's expectations. He highlighted the importance of improving execution across all segments as a key priority moving forward. The company is focused on building a leaner organization, having made significant progress in reducing its selling, general, and administrative (SG&A) expenses. As Mammoth Energy looks ahead to 2026, there is optimism about the potential for growth driven by internal initiatives, favorable market conditions, and a commitment to deploying capital into high-return opportunities.

Overall, Mammoth Energy's strategic decisions in 2025 have positioned the company for future growth, despite the challenges faced in Q4. The focus on operational efficiency and investment in scalable businesses reflects a proactive approach to enhancing shareholder value. With a solid balance sheet and a clear strategy, Mammoth Energy aims to unlock the value within its operations and deliver improved results in the coming years.
social_text: Mammoth Energy reports Q4 EPS of 26c vs. (20c) last year, with $9.5M revenue. CFO Mark Layton highlights $150M from divestitures and $65M invested in aviation, aiming for long-term shareholder value.

title: 23-year-old inherited $450K, parked it and doesn't know what to do next. What Dave Ramsey says will get the 'most lift'
summary:
In a recent discussion, financial expert Dave Ramsey addressed the situation of a 23-year-old who inherited $450,000 but is unsure of how to manage it effectively. This scenario highlights a common challenge faced by young adults who suddenly come into significant wealth. Ramsey emphasized the importance of investing early, as time is a crucial factor in wealth accumulation. He pointed out that if the inherited money were invested at long-term market rates, it could potentially double in about seven years, contrasting this with the low yields offered by traditional savings methods like certificates of deposit (CDs).

Ramsey praised the young individual for initially parking the money in a CD, which helped him avoid impulsive spending. However, he cautioned that simply letting the money sit without making a strategic investment could be detrimental due to inflation and missed opportunities for growth. The discussion underscored the psychological aspects of managing sudden wealth, where decision paralysis can occur, leading to inaction. Ramsey's advice serves as a reminder that while caution is necessary, proactive investment strategies are essential for long-term financial success.

The conversation also shed light on the broader context of wealth distribution among younger Americans, where the median net worth for those under 35 is significantly lower than that of older generations. This disparity emphasizes the need for financial education and strategic planning among young adults. Ramsey's insights aim to empower young inheritors to make informed decisions that leverage their time and resources effectively, ultimately fostering a mindset geared towards growth and financial independence.
social_text: A 23-year-old inherited $450K but feels stuck. Dave Ramsey advises investing early to combat inflation and maximize growth, as time is a powerful ally in wealth accumulation. Don't let decision paralysis hold you back.

title: Howard Marks says don’t let war ‘affect your emotions’ around investing. How to stay the course during turbulent times
summary:
In a recent discussion, Howard Marks emphasized the importance of maintaining emotional stability during turbulent market conditions, particularly in light of ongoing global conflicts. He cautioned investors against common pitfalls such as panic selling, over-concentration in asset classes like commodities, and remaining in cash for extended periods while waiting for market stability. Marks highlighted that emotional reactions to market volatility can lead to significant underperformance compared to those who remain invested and maintain a diversified portfolio. This advice is particularly relevant as investors grapple with the implications of geopolitical tensions on financial markets.

Marks pointed out that uncertainty can drive investors to make hasty decisions that could lock in losses, which long-term investors might otherwise recover from. He referenced research from Vanguard, which indicates that emotional responses to market fluctuations often result in poorer investment outcomes. The current geopolitical climate, including the ongoing conflict and its potential impact on oil prices and credit markets, adds to the complexity of investment decisions. Marks urged investors to focus on the unknowns rather than succumbing to fear-driven actions.

During his appearance at the Australian Financial Review Business Summit, Marks reiterated the unpredictable nature of current events, stating that no one can accurately forecast the duration or consequences of the ongoing conflict. He underscored the importance of recognizing the limits of our knowledge in these situations. As investors navigate these uncertain waters, the key takeaway is to remain calm, avoid emotional trading, and stick to a well-thought-out investment strategy that prioritizes diversification and long-term growth.
social_text: Howard Marks advises against emotional investing during turbulent times, warning that panic selling can lock in losses. Historical data shows that staying invested and diversified leads to better outcomes amid uncertainty.

title: Artelo Biosciences announces 3-for-1 reverse stock split
summary:
Artelo Biosciences has announced a significant corporate action in the form of a 3-for-1 reverse stock split, which will take effect on February 27, 2026. This decision was made by the Board of Directors with the intention of enhancing the marketability and liquidity of the company's common stock. By consolidating shares, the company aims to increase the price per share, which can potentially attract a broader range of investors and improve trading volume on the Nasdaq Capital Market.

The reverse stock split will result in shareholders receiving one new share for every three shares they currently own. This adjustment is scheduled to take effect when the market opens on March 10, 2026. Such corporate actions are often employed by companies to meet minimum share price requirements for continued listing on stock exchanges or to make shares more appealing to institutional investors who may have minimum price thresholds for investment.

Artelo's decision reflects a strategic move to strengthen its position in the market and potentially enhance shareholder value. While reverse stock splits can sometimes be viewed negatively by investors, as they may signal underlying issues, in this case, the company is positioning itself for future growth and stability in a competitive sector. The effectiveness of this strategy will depend on subsequent market performance and investor sentiment following the split.
social_text: Artelo Biosciences announces a 3-for-1 reverse stock split effective February 27, 2026, to boost share price and market liquidity, trading on a split-adjusted basis from March 10, 2026.

title: Alpha Modus resolves patent infringement litigation against Brookshire
summary:
Alpha Modus (AMOD) has successfully resolved its patent infringement litigation against Brookshire Grocery Co., which was pending in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas. The litigation centered around allegations that Brookshire infringed on Alpha Modus’s patented technologies, which encompass a range of innovative solutions including real-time consumer behavior analysis, digital advertising systems, smart retail display technologies, and AI-driven in-store engagement platforms. These technologies are crucial for enhancing customer experiences and optimizing retail operations in physical environments.

The resolution of this litigation marks a significant milestone for Alpha Modus, allowing the company to focus on its core business without the distraction of ongoing legal disputes. By moving for dismissal of the litigation with prejudice, both parties have agreed to resolve all claims, which prevents any future litigation on the same issues. This outcome not only reinforces Alpha Modus's intellectual property rights but also underscores the importance of innovation in the retail sector, where technology plays a pivotal role in shaping consumer interactions.

As Alpha Modus continues to develop and deploy its advanced technologies, the resolution of this case may enhance its competitive position in the market. The company can now redirect its resources towards further innovation and expansion, potentially leading to new partnerships and opportunities in the rapidly evolving landscape of retail technology. This development is a positive indicator for stakeholders and investors, as it reflects the company's commitment to protecting its intellectual property while fostering growth in a dynamic industry.
social_text: Alpha Modus resolves patent litigation against Brookshire Grocery Co. concerning real-time consumer behavior analysis and AI-driven retail technologies, allowing AMOD to focus on innovation and growth.

title: Goldman likes these five stocks as market fears intensify
summary:

The investment bank has recommended buying the dip in companies such as Nvidia, Ross Stores, Viking Holdings, Once Upon a Farm, and Dutch Bros. Each of these companies has shown resilience and potential for growth, making them attractive options for investors looking to navigate the current market volatility. Analyst Christine Cho highlighted Dutch Bros as a standout, upgrading its rating from neutral to buy. She emphasized the coffee chain's strong growth trajectory, driven by impressive same-store sales and unit economics, despite a recent 16% decline in its stock price.

Once Upon a Farm also received a buy rating from analyst Leah Jordan, who initiated coverage on the children's food brand. Goldman Sachs noted the company's strong brand power and market position, particularly in the context of the growing trend towards healthier consumption. Jordan praised the management's robust brand strategy and highlighted the company's leadership in dollar growth within its categories, suggesting solid pricing power due to low elasticity to previous price increases. This positions Once Upon a Farm favorably for future growth.

Additionally, Viking Holdings has been recommended for purchase following a strong earnings report. Analyst Lizzie Dove expressed optimism about the luxury cruise company's performance and potential upside. Overall, Goldman Sachs' recommendations reflect a strategic approach to investing in companies that are well-positioned to thrive in the current economic climate, providing investors with opportunities to capitalize on market dips and emerging trends.

title: Prediction Markets Are Here to Stay, but This Stock Is a Better Way to Play the Trend
summary:

These markets allow users to bet on the outcomes of various events, including political elections and entertainment awards. The surge in popularity is evident, as companies like DraftKings have expanded their offerings to include prediction markets, while stock trading platforms like Robinhood and Interactive Brokers have also entered this space. This trend indicates a growing acceptance and integration of prediction markets into mainstream finance and entertainment, suggesting they are likely to remain a fixture in the market landscape.

The rise of artificial intelligence (AI) is further influencing prediction markets, with users employing agentic AI to interact with these platforms. This innovative approach allows for a more sophisticated betting experience, akin to having a digital bookie. However, the leading prediction market companies, Kalshi and Polymarket, remain private, limiting direct investment opportunities. As the market evolves, the intersection of AI and prediction markets presents new avenues for growth and investment.

Investors looking to capitalize on this trend may consider Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) as a strategic play. TSMC is positioned as a critical supplier for the tech industry, providing the semiconductors that power the servers and devices used in prediction markets and AI applications. As the demand for these technologies continues to rise, TSMC's role as a foundational player in the semiconductor space makes it a compelling investment for those looking to benefit from the growth of prediction markets and AI-driven innovations.

title: TX woman is inheriting $200K and wonders whether to pay off her house or invest. The Ramsey Show says ‘buy time’ instead
summary:
In a recent episode of The Ramsey Show, a Texas woman reached out for financial advice regarding her impending inheritance of $200,000. The couple, who earn approximately $150,000 annually, are contemplating whether to pay off their mortgage or invest the funds. With a remaining mortgage balance of $320,000 on a $340,000 home and the uncertainty of their long-term housing plans, they are seeking guidance on how to best utilize their newfound wealth. The hosts, George Kamel and Jade Warshaw, emphasized the importance of stabilizing finances before making long-term investment decisions, aligning their advice with Dave Ramsey's financial principles.

The hosts recommended that the couple first establish an emergency fund covering six months of expenses, which is crucial for financial stability. They also suggested eliminating any non-housing debt, as paying off high-interest loans can yield a guaranteed return equivalent to the interest rate on that debt. This approach is designed to provide a solid financial foundation before considering more complex investment strategies.

Ultimately, the discussion highlighted the significance of prioritizing financial security over immediate investment opportunities. With the couple's potential move within three years, the hosts advised caution in making significant changes to their mortgage situation. Instead, they encouraged a focus on building a robust financial plan that accommodates both short-term needs and long-term goals, ensuring that the couple can make informed decisions about their financial future.
social_text: A Texas couple with a $200K inheritance is advised to stabilize finances first. With $320K left on their mortgage, they should build an emergency fund and eliminate high-interest debt before investing.

title: Telecom stocks have had a great start to the year — and they’re still quite cheap
summary:
Telecom stocks have shown a robust performance at the start of the year, with investors gaining confidence in the competitive dynamics of the industry. Concerns from late last year regarding Verizon's growth strategy under new CEO Dan Schulman have subsided, as Wall Street now anticipates a more disciplined approach to pricing. This shift in sentiment has contributed to a broader momentum for value stocks, with the Russell 3000 Value Index rising by 3.3% while the Growth Index has seen a decline of 5.5%. 

Among the major players in the U.S. wireless telecom market, T-Mobile US stands out with a forward price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of 20.2, reflecting its expected revenue growth outpacing that of AT&T and Verizon. The market shares for these companies are closely contested, with T-Mobile holding 35%, Verizon at 34%, and AT&T at 27% as of 2024. This competitive landscape indicates a healthy rivalry that could benefit consumers and investors alike.

Despite T-Mobile's higher valuation, other telecom stocks like Verizon and AT&T are considered deep-value plays, trading at forward P/E ratios significantly lower than the S&P 500 communications sector average. Verizon and AT&T are among the least expensive stocks in their sector, which could present attractive investment opportunities. Additionally, cable providers such as Charter Communications and Comcast have rebounded strongly, with year-to-date rallies of 11.3% and 14%, respectively, indicating a positive shift in market perception towards these companies as well.
